title: The Chiefs' offseason board is live.
lede: The site now keeps a human-curated layer above the live feeds: the roster reset, schedule watch, rookie class and account list Chiefs fans should keep open right now.

facts.label: Current frame
facts.detail: NFL.com lists Kansas City coming off a 6-11 season and 3rd place in the AFC West, making the 2026 offseason a real recalibration.

facts.label: Schedule status
facts.detail: Chiefs.com has the 2026 home and away opponents posted; the full league schedule release is still the next major calendar event.

facts.label: Draft class
facts.detail: The class starts with Mansoor Delane, Peter Woods and R Mason Thomas, then adds Jadon Canady, Emmett Johnson, Cyrus Allen and Garrett Nussmeier.

storylines.label: Mahomes watch
storylines.title: Rehab timeline, install plan, backup-floor math
storylines.detail: The best coverage separates real medical updates from fan anxiety. Track official language, OTA participation and what the offense can install around managed reps.

storylines.label: Kelce returns
storylines.title: The middle of the field still runs through 87
storylines.detail: Kelce's return keeps the Chiefs' best spacing answers available, but the bigger question is how Kansas City protects his workload while rebuilding offensive rhythm.

storylines.label: Walker role
storylines.title: More receiving usage could change the down-to-down math
storylines.detail: Kenneth Walker's passing-game role is one of the cleanest early tells for whether the Chiefs are serious about easier offense and a higher weekly floor.

storylines.label: Rookie map
storylines.title: Defense-first draft class needs defined jobs fast
storylines.detail: Delane, Woods, Thomas and Canady give Steve Spagnuolo new chess pieces. The early question is not who starts, but who owns a useful package by September.
